Authentication Method:
ICS LDAP
User Role Detection:
Automatic based on LDAP accountType
Data Updates:
Daily synchronization with Registrar files
Access Control:
Role-based permissions (faculty, student, administrator)
Student Search:
Real-time LDAP lookup by ID or name
Network Requirements:
On-campus access or UCI VPN connection required

Checkin
Overview
CheckIn is a web-based attendance tracking system designed for the UCI community that uses geolocation verification to confirm student presence in classes and meetings. Students access CheckIn through direct course links provided by professors or by visiting checkin.ics.uci.edu, where they authenticate via web auth and enter a daily “word of the day” provided by their instructor. The system automatically attempts to determine the student's geolocation through their browser, requiring permission to access location information for verification purposes. Students can view their complete check-in history for the current quarter through the “My Attendance” tab, which displays all course activity and session participation records.
For instructors, CheckIn provides comprehensive course management tools including the ability to create daily words (active for specified durations from 5 minutes to midnight), import bulk word lists for entire quarters, manage course rosters, and excuse absences. The system generates multiple report formats including downloadable attendance sheets, online attendance lists, visual graphs, and Google Maps-based location verification. CheckIn integrates seamlessly with Canvas LMS, allowing instructors to either import/export grade reports or directly publish attendance data to Canvas assignments through an authentication token system. The platform also supports meeting attendance tracking for organizers and includes role-based access for different user types (students, instructors, head instructors, department analysts, and meeting organizers), with each role having appropriate permissions and functionality tailored to their needs.

How to Check In
Connect to UCI WiFi (
REQUIRED
)
If you use an Apple device,

– otherwise you may not be able to access the website, or your location may be detected incorrectly
Open the
Checkin@UCI
link or scan the QR code during class
Log in with your UCI account
Select your course
Enter the word of the day (case sensitive)
Allow location access when prompted (the system will automatically detect your location)
Submit your check-in
Important Notes
The
word of the day is case sensitive
(uppercase/lowercase must match exactly)
You must allow
location access
; otherwise your check-in will fail
Check-in is time-sensitive
, so make sure you submit during class (or the timeframe specified by your instructor)
Incorrect location or checking in at the wrong time may result in your check-in being invalid
Location and Device Issues
For best success, please make sure you connect to UCI WiFi. If you are not using UCI WiFi, you may not be able to access the website.
Apple users: please
turn off iCloud Private Relay
to ensure correct address detection.
You could test your location settings in advance:
Test Geoloation
Troubleshooting
Q. Students are receiving 403 errors
Students may receive a 403 error if they are coming from an unexpected IP address.
Troubleshooting Steps
Confirm that the address in the grey bar is an on-campus address. The table of on-campus addresses is available here:
Campus IP Address Space
If the address is not in the above space,
the student must connect to UCI Wireless or the Campus VPN before connecting to checkin
.
If the user is on an Apple device, see the troubleshooting section about
Apple Private Relay
If the address is in the Campus IP Address Space, please click on the Copy Details button and paste that information in an email to helpdesk@ics.uci.edu.
Q. Apple  Private Relay: Students on Apple Devices Receiving 403 errors
Apple has enabled a feature called
iCloud Private Relay
by default. This feature hides the device's IP address and causes connections to Checkin to appear as if they are coming from off-campus IP addresses owned by CloudFlare (104.28.0.0/16).
As of 15:30 on 10/5/2025, Checkin has been updated to temporarily accept connections from these networks.
Long term, please ask students to follow the guidance from Apple at the following link to mitigate the issue:
Apple KB Article: Private Relay

Deliveries
All Deliveries
All deliveries going to the ICS Computing Support group should be shipped to the following address:
Your Name
c/o ICS Computing Support
302 Inner Ring Road, Suite 346
Computer Science (CS) Building
Irvine, CA 92697-3425
Medium to Large Deliveries
Medium to large deliveries are typically equipment that arrives on a pallet or vis carrier
These deliveries must be scheduled
at least one day prior to delivery and delivered to the Engineering Tower Loading Dock. Deliveries can be scheduled by sending an email to helpdesk@ics.uci.edu. Please include an ETA, the name of the shipping company, and the dispatch or driver phone number if available.
Scheduling the delivery ensures that a member from the Computing Support Group is present to accept the delivery and move it to secure storage in the data center.
If carriers are unable to complete delivery or are forced to perform inside deliveries, additional carrier charges may accrue, and these charges will be billed against the PO.
Directions to the Engineering Tower Loading Docks
When making medium to large purchases that will need to be delivered via the loading dock, please provide the following special directions to the carrier:
The loading docks are attached to Engineering Tower (Building 303 in quadrant 6E on the
Campus Map.
)
Closest cross streets are Peltason Drive and Engineering Service Road.
Loading docks are at the end of Engineering Service Road.

ICS Open Lab in CS 364
ICS  Openlab in  CS 364
The CS 364 Openlab is a large, dedicated space consisting of
two connected labs
offering
over 4,000 square feet with 120 seats
. It provides a quiet, comfortable environment ideal for a variety of activities, including:
TA meetings and office hours
Club meetings
Group study sessions
Student collaboration and project work
Individual study
Note:
At certain times during the day, one of the two labs is dedicated to laptop instruction. During these times, the other lab remains available for general student use. Please check the schedule or contact the lab for current laptop lab hours. See the most up to date scheduling here:
Laptop Instructional Lab Schedule
Details
Access:
First-come, first-served basis (no reservation system at this time)
Staffing:
Lab is staffed from noon until closing
Food/Beverage Policy:
Light snacks and beverages in spill-proof containers are welcome
Features
Expansive Footprint: 4,000 sq. ft. of well-lit flexible workspace engineered for comfort and peak productivity.
Versatile Seating: A wide variety of seating configurations to support both heads-down solo work and dynamic group projects.
Ubiquitous Power: Integrated power access throughout the lab, ensuring your laptops and mobile devices stay charged anywhere you sit.
Collaborative Ecosystem: An open-concept layout designed to facilitate teamwork, peer learning, and spontaneous innovation.
Focused Zones: Dedicated quiet areas for students requiring a distraction-free environment for intensive individual study.
Designated Break Areas: Convenient access to the
downstairs patio
and the
Southwest elevator bay
for meals and social breaks.
CS 364 Openlab Hours
Regular Schedule

Hours

8:00 AM to 10:00 PM
Saturday - Sunday

Note:
This space is
CLOSED
on all holidays, weekends and during quarter breaks.  Alternate hours may be posted below during finals and welcome week.

364
The lab is easily accessible from the either elevator or stairs on the third floor of the original Computer Science building. You can't miss it, it occupies nearly half the floor.
Lab Policies
Lab Policies and Guidlines
Food and Drink Policy
We recognize that students spend many hours in the Openlab and may need a snack or beverage while studying. To maintain a professional and comfortable environment for everyone, please adhere to the following guidelines:
Snacks are Welcome:
Light snack foods are permitted,
in the CS 364 Openlab on
ly, provided they are kept away from computing equipment.
Heavy or Aromatic Meals:
To keep the lab environment fresh and inclusive,
heavy meals or foods with significant aromas
(e.g., hot takeout or large meals) are not permitted inside the lab. Please enjoy these items on the
downstairs patio
or in the
Southwest elevator bay
.
Beverages:
Drinks must be in spill-proof containers with secure, twist-off, or snap-on lids.
Cleanliness:
Please wipe up any crumbs or minor spills. Dispose of all food-related trash and recyclables in the
hallway or restroom receptacles
rather than the lab bins to prevent odors. Additional cleaning supplies are available at the lab attendant station.
No-Fault Policy:
Accidents happen! Please notify a lab attendant or ICS Computing Support staff member immediately if a spill occurs. Prompt reporting ensures that equipment and furniture are treated correctly to prevent permanent damage.
ICS Computing Support reserves the right to ask people not following this policy to relocate to finish their food or drinks or to put them away.
ICS Open Lab 364 | Computer Science Building | University of California, Irvine
For more information, visit the
ICS Computing Support website

ICS Computing Support
The
ICS Computing Support team
serves the School of Information and Computer Sciences by delivering innovative technology solutions and leadership that enhance instructional and research activities. We maintain a reliable, scalable compute environment and provide unified IT support to faculty, students, and staff across the entire ICS community and associated ORUs.
The
ICS Computing Support Wiki
is a centralized resource for faculty, students, and staff within the Donald Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences at UC Irvine. Its primary purpose is to provide documentation and technical assistance for the school’s specific computing environment.
ICS Open Lab in CS 364
, a valuable resource available to support student learning, collaboration, and innovation within the ICS community.

Trace: • Formatting Syntax Formatting Syntax Table of Contents Formatting Syntax Basic Text Formatting Links External Internal Interwiki Windows Shares Image Links Footnotes Sectioning Headline Level 3 Images and Other Files Lists Text Conversions Text to Image Conversions Text to HTML Conversions Quoting Tables No Formatting Code Blocks Syntax Highlighting Downloadable Code Blocks Embedding HTML and PHP RSS/ATOM Feed Aggregation Control Macros Syntax Plugins Formatting Syntax DokuWiki supports some simple markup language, which tries to make the datafiles to be as readable as possible. This page contains all possible syntax you may use when editing the pages. Simply have a look at the source of this page by pressing “Edit this page”. If you want to try something, just use the playground page. The simpler markup is easily accessible via quickbuttons , too. Basic Text Formatting DokuWiki supports bold , italic , underlined and monospaced texts. Of course you can combine all these. DokuWiki supports **bold**, //italic//, __underlined__ and ''monospaced'' texts. Of course you can **__//''combine''//__** all these. You can use subscript and superscript , too. You can use subscript and superscript, too. You can mark something as deleted as well. You can mark something as deleted as well. Paragraphs are created from blank lines. If you want to force a newline without a paragraph, you can use two backslashes followed by a whitespace or the end of line. This is some text with some linebreaks Note that the two backslashes are only recognized at the end of a line or followed by a whitespace \\this happens without it. This is some text with some linebreaks\\ Note that the two backslashes are only recognized at the end of a line\\ or followed by\\ a whitespace \\this happens without it. You should use forced newlines only if really needed. Links DokuWiki supports multiple ways of creating links. External External links are recognized automagically: http://www.google.com or simply www.google.com - You can set the link text as well: This Link points to google . Email addresses like this one: andi@splitbrain.org are recognized, too. DokuWiki supports multiple ways of creating links. External links are recognized automagically: http://www.google.com or simply www.google.com - You can set link text as well: [[http://www.google.com|This Link points to google]]. Email addresses like this one: are recognized, too. Internal Internal links are created by using square brackets. You can either just give a pagename or use an additional link text . Internal links are created by using square brackets. You can either just give a [[pagename]] or use an additional [[pagename|link text]]. Wiki pagenames are converted to lowercase automatically, special characters are not allowed. You can use namespaces by using a colon in the pagename. You can use [[some:namespaces]] by using a colon in the pagename. For details about namespaces see namespaces . Linking to a specific section is possible, too. Just add the section name behind a hash character as known from HTML . This links to this Section . This links to [[syntax#internal|this Section]]. Notes: Links to existing pages are shown in a different style from nonexisting ones. DokuWiki does not use CamelCase to automatically create links by default, but this behavior can be enabled in the config file. Hint: If DokuWiki is a link, then it's enabled. When a section's heading is changed, its bookmark changes, too. So don't rely on section linking too much. Interwiki DokuWiki supports Interwiki links. These are quick links to other Wikis. For example this is a link to Wikipedia's page about Wikis: Wiki . DokuWiki supports [[doku>Interwiki]] links. These are quick links to other Wikis. For example this is a link to Wikipedia's page about Wikis: [[wp>Wiki]]. Windows Shares Windows shares like this are recognized, too. Please note that these only make sense in a homogeneous user group like a corporate Intranet . Windows Shares like [[\\server\share|this]] are recognized, too. Notes: For security reasons direct browsing of windows shares only works in Microsoft Internet Explorer per default (and only in the “local zone”). For Mozilla and Firefox it can be enabled through different workaround mentioned in the Mozilla Knowledge Base . However, there will still be a JavaScript warning about trying to open a Windows Share. To remove this warning (for all users), put the following line in conf/userscript.js : LANG.nosmblinks = ''; Image Links You can also use an image to link to another internal or external page by combining the syntax for links and images (see below) like this: [[http://www.php.net|{{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}}]] Please note: The image formatting is the only formatting syntax accepted in link names. The whole image and link syntax is supported (including image resizing, internal and external images and URLs and interwiki links). Footnotes You can add footnotes 1) by using double parentheses. You can add footnotes ((This is a footnote)) by using double parentheses. Sectioning You can use up to five different levels of headlines to structure your content. If you have more than three headlines, a table of contents is generated automatically – this can be disabled by including the string ~~NOTOC~~ in the document. Headline Level 3 Headline Level 4 Headline Level 5 ==== Headline Level 3 ==== === Headline Level 4 === == Headline Level 5 == By using four or more dashes, you can make a horizontal line: Images and Other Files You can include external and internal images with curly brackets. Optionally you can specify the size of them. Real size: Resize to given width: Resize to given width and height 2) : Resized external image: Real size: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}} Resize to given width: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png?50}} Resize to given width and height: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png?200x50}} Resized external image: {{http://de3.php.net/images/php.gif?200x50}} By using left or right whitespaces you can choose the alignment. {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}} {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png }} {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png }} Of course, you can add a title (displayed as a tooltip by most browsers), too. {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png |This is the caption}} If you specify a filename (external or internal) that is not an image ( gif, jpeg, png ), then it will be displayed as a link instead. For linking an image to another page see Image Links above. Lists Dokuwiki supports ordered and unordered lists. To create a list item, indent your text by two spaces and use a * for unordered lists or a - for ordered ones. This is a list The second item You may have different levels Another item The same list but ordered Another item Just use indention for deeper levels That's it * This is a list * The second item * You may have different levels * Another item - The same list but ordered - Another item - Just use indention for deeper levels - That's it Also take a look at the FAQ on list items . Text Conversions DokuWiki can convert certain pre-defined characters or strings into images or other text or HTML . The text to image conversion is mainly done for smileys. And the text to HTML conversion is used for typography replacements, but can be configured to use other HTML as well. Text to Image Conversions DokuWiki converts commonly used emoticon s to their graphical equivalents. Those Smileys and other images can be configured and extended. Here is an overview of Smileys included in DokuWiki: 8-) 8-O :-( :-) =) :-/ :-\ :-? :-D :-P :-O :-X :-| ;-) ^_^ :?: :!: LOL FIXME DELETEME Text to HTML Conversions Typography: DokuWiki can convert simple text characters to their typographically correct entities. Here is an example of recognized characters. → ← ↔ ⇒ ⇐ ⇔ » « – — 640×480 © ™ ® “He thought 'It's a man's world'…” -> <- <-> => <= <=> >> << -- --- 640x480 (c) (tm) (r) "He thought 'It's a man's world'..." The same can be done to produce any kind of HTML , it just needs to be added to the pattern file . There are three exceptions which do not come from that pattern file: multiplication entity (640×480), 'single' and “double quotes”. They can be turned off through a config option . Quoting Some times you want to mark some text to show it's a reply or comment. You can use the following syntax: I think we should do it > No we shouldn't >> Well, I say we should > Really? >> Yes! >>> Then lets do it! I think we should do it No we shouldn't Well, I say we should Really? Yes! Then lets do it! Tables DokuWiki supports a simple syntax to create tables. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 1 Row 1 Col 2 Row 1 Col 3 Row 2 Col 1 some colspan (note the double pipe) Row 3 Col 1 Row 3 Col 2 Row 3 Col 3 Table rows have to start and end with a | for normal rows or a ^ for headers. ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ Heading 3 ^ | Row 1 Col 1 | Row 1 Col 2 | Row 1 Col 3 | | Row 2 Col 1 | some colspan (note the double pipe) || | Row 3 Col 1 | Row 3 Col 2 | Row 3 Col 3 | To connect cells horizontally, just make the next cell completely empty as shown above. Be sure to have always the same amount of cell separators! Vertical tableheaders are possible, too. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 2 Row 1 Col 3 Heading 4 no colspan this time Heading 5 Row 2 Col 2 Row 2 Col 3 As you can see, it's the cell separator before a cell which decides about the formatting: | ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ ^ Heading 3 | Row 1 Col 2 | Row 1 Col 3 | ^ Heading 4 | no colspan this time | | ^ Heading 5 | Row 2 Col 2 | Row 2 Col 3 | You can have rowspans (vertically connected cells) by adding ::: into the cells below the one to which they should connect. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 1 this cell spans vertically Row 1 Col 3 Row 2 Col 1 Row 2 Col 3 Row 3 Col 1 Row 2 Col 3 Apart from the rowspan syntax those cells should not contain anything else. ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ Heading 3 ^ | Row 1 Col 1 | this cell spans vertically | Row 1 Col 3 | | Row 2 Col 1 | ::: | Row 2 Col 3 | | Row 3 Col 1 | ::: | Row 2 Col 3 | You can align the table contents, too. Just add at least two whitespaces at the opposite end of your text: Add two spaces on the left to align right, two spaces on the right to align left and two spaces at least at both ends for centered text. Table with alignment right center left left right center xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxx This is how it looks in the source: ^ Table with alignment ^^^ | right| center |left | |left | right| center | | xxxxxxxxxxxx | xxxxxxxxxxxx | xxxxxxxxxxxx | Note: Vertical alignment is not supported. No Formatting If you need to display text exactly like it is typed (without any formatting), enclose the area either with tags or even simpler, with double percent signs %% . This is some text which contains addresses like this: http://www.splitbrain.org and **formatting**, but nothing is done with it. The same is true for //__this__ text// with a smiley ;-). This is some text which contains addresses like this: http://www.splitbrain.org and **formatting**, but nothing is done with it. The same is true for %%//__this__ text// with a smiley ;-)%%. Code Blocks You can include code blocks into your documents by either indenting them by at least two spaces (like used for the previous examples) or by using the tags or . This is text is indented by two spaces. This is preformatted code all spaces are preserved: like <-this This is pretty much the same, but you could use it to show that you quoted a file. Those blocks were created by this source: This is text is indented by two spaces. This is preformatted code all spaces are preserved: like <-this This is pretty much the same, but you could use it to show that you quoted a file. Syntax Highlighting DokuWiki can highlight sourcecode, which makes it easier to read. It uses the GeSHi Generic Syntax Highlighter – so any language supported by GeSHi is supported. The syntax uses the same code and file blocks described in the previous section, but this time the name of the language syntax to be highlighted is included inside the tag, e.g. or . /** * The HelloWorldApp class implements an application that * simply displays "Hello World!" to the standard output. */ class HelloWorldApp { public static void main ( String [ ] args ) { System . out . println ( "Hello World!" ) ; //Display the string. } } The following language strings are currently recognized: 4cs, 6502acme, 6502kickass, 6502tasm, 68000devpac, abap, actionscript-french, actionscript, actionscript3, ada, algol68, apache, applescript, asm, asp, autoconf, autohotkey, autoit, avisynth, awk, bascomavr, bash, basic4gl, bf, bibtex, blitzbasic, bnf, boo, c, c_loadrunner, c_mac, caddcl, cadlisp, cfdg, cfm, chaiscript, cil, clojure, cmake, cobol, coffeescript, cpp, cpp-qt, csharp, css, cuesheet, d, dcs, delphi, diff, div, dos, dot, e, epc, ecmascript, eiffel, email, erlang, euphoria, f1, falcon, fo, fortran, freebasic, fsharp, gambas, genero, genie, gdb, glsl, gml, gnuplot, go, groovy, gettext, gwbasic, haskell, hicest, hq9plus, html, html5, icon, idl, ini, inno, intercal, io, j, java5, java, javascript, jquery, kixtart, klonec, klonecpp, latex, lb, lisp, llvm, locobasic, logtalk, lolcode, lotusformulas, lotusscript, lscript, lsl2, lua, m68k, magiksf, make, mapbasic, matlab, mirc, modula2, modula3, mmix, mpasm, mxml, mysql, newlisp, nsis, oberon2, objc, objeck, ocaml-brief, ocaml, oobas, oracle8, oracle11, oxygene, oz, pascal, pcre, perl, perl6, per, pf, php-brief, php, pike, pic16, pixelbender, pli, plsql, postgresql, povray, powerbuilder, powershell, proftpd, progress, prolog, properties, providex, purebasic, pycon, python, q, qbasic, rails, rebol, reg, robots, rpmspec, rsplus, ruby, sas, scala, scheme, scilab, sdlbasic, smalltalk, smarty, sql, systemverilog, tcl, teraterm, text, thinbasic, tsql, typoscript, unicon, uscript, vala, vbnet, vb, verilog, vhdl, vim, visualfoxpro, visualprolog, whitespace, winbatch, whois, xbasic, xml, xorg_conf, xpp, yaml, z80, zxbasic Downloadable Code Blocks When you use the or syntax as above, you might want to make the shown code available for download as well. You can do this by specifying a file name after language code like this: myexample.php If you don't want any highlighting but want a downloadable file, specify a dash ( - ) as the language code: . Embedding HTML and PHP You can embed raw HTML or PHP code into your documents by using the or tags. (Use uppercase tags if you need to enclose block level elements.) HTML example: This is some inline HTML

And this is some block HTML

This is some inline HTML And this is some block HTML PHP example: echo 'A logo generated by PHP:'; echo ''; echo '(generated inline HTML)'; echo ''; echo ''; echo '
The same, but inside a block level element:
'; echo 'A logo generated by PHP:'; echo ''; echo '(inline HTML )'; echo ''; echo ''; echo '
The same, but inside a block level element:
'; Please Note : HTML and PHP embedding is disabled by default in the configuration. If disabled, the code is displayed instead of executed. RSS/ATOM Feed Aggregation DokuWiki can integrate data from external XML feeds. For parsing the XML feeds, SimplePie is used. All formats understood by SimplePie can be used in DokuWiki as well. You can influence the rendering by multiple additional space separated parameters: Parameter Description any number will be used as maximum number items to show, defaults to 8 reverse display the last items in the feed first author show item authors names date show item dates description show the item description. If HTML is disabled all tags will be stripped n [dhm] refresh period, where d=days, h=hours, m=minutes. (e.g. 12h = 12 hours). The refresh period defaults to 4 hours. Any value below 10 minutes will be treated as 10 minutes. DokuWiki will generally try to supply a cached version of a page, obviously this is inappropriate when the page contains dynamic external content. The parameter tells DokuWiki to re-render the page if it is more than refresh period since the page was last rendered. Example: {{rss>http://slashdot.org/index.rss 5 author date 1h }} Control Macros Some syntax influences how DokuWiki renders a page without creating any output it self. The following control macros are availble: Macro Description ~~NOTOC~~ If this macro is found on the page, no table of contents will be created ~~NOCACHE~~ DokuWiki caches all output by default. Sometimes this might not be wanted (eg. when the syntax above is used), adding this macro will force DokuWiki to rerender a page on every call Syntax Plugins DokuWiki's syntax can be extended by Plugins . How the installed plugins are used is described on their appropriate description pages. The following syntax plugins are available in this particular DokuWiki installation: Add New Page plugin 2025-08-03 by Damien Regad, Benjamin Santalucia, Sam Wilson and other contributors Adds a "new page form" to any wiki page. config:htmlok Plugin 2023-07-12 by Luis Machuca Bezzaza Enables raw HTML like the old 'config:htmlok' behaviour. Dw2Pdf plugin 2023-11-25 by Andreas Gohr and Luigi Micco DokuWiki to PDF converter Gallery Plugin 2024-04-30 by Andreas Gohr Creates a gallery of images from a namespace or RSS/ATOM feed Indexmenu Plugin 2024-01-05 by Samuele Tognini, Gerrit Uitslag Show a customizable and sortable index for a namespace Info Plugin 2020-06-04 by Andreas Gohr Displays information about various DokuWiki internals Note Plugin 2024-04-15 by Anael Mobilia Add Note/Important/Tip/Warning Capability (DIV+CSS box) slider plugin 2020-07-08 by Andreas Gohr Add a slider element to any DokuWiki page Wrap Plugin 2023-08-13 by Anika Henke Universal plugin which combines functionalities of many other plugins. Wrap wiki text inside containers (divs or spans) and give them a class (choose from a variety of preset classes), a width and/or a language with its associated text direction. 1) This is a footnote 2) when the aspect ratio of the given width and height doesn't match that of the image, it will be cropped to the new ratio before resizing

Trace: • Formatting Syntax Formatting Syntax Table of Contents Formatting Syntax Basic Text Formatting Links External Internal Interwiki Windows Shares Image Links Footnotes Sectioning Headline Level 3 Images and Other Files Lists Text Conversions Text to Image Conversions Text to HTML Conversions Quoting Tables No Formatting Code Blocks Syntax Highlighting Downloadable Code Blocks Embedding HTML and PHP RSS/ATOM Feed Aggregation Control Macros Syntax Plugins Formatting Syntax DokuWiki supports some simple markup language, which tries to make the datafiles to be as readable as possible. This page contains all possible syntax you may use when editing the pages. Simply have a look at the source of this page by pressing “Edit this page”. If you want to try something, just use the playground page. The simpler markup is easily accessible via quickbuttons , too. Basic Text Formatting DokuWiki supports bold , italic , underlined and monospaced texts. Of course you can combine all these. DokuWiki supports **bold**, //italic//, __underlined__ and ''monospaced'' texts. Of course you can **__//''combine''//__** all these. You can use subscript and superscript , too. You can use subscript and superscript, too. You can mark something as deleted as well. You can mark something as deleted as well. Paragraphs are created from blank lines. If you want to force a newline without a paragraph, you can use two backslashes followed by a whitespace or the end of line. This is some text with some linebreaks Note that the two backslashes are only recognized at the end of a line or followed by a whitespace \\this happens without it. This is some text with some linebreaks\\ Note that the two backslashes are only recognized at the end of a line\\ or followed by\\ a whitespace \\this happens without it. You should use forced newlines only if really needed. Links DokuWiki supports multiple ways of creating links. External External links are recognized automagically: http://www.google.com or simply www.google.com - You can set the link text as well: This Link points to google . Email addresses like this one: andi@splitbrain.org are recognized, too. DokuWiki supports multiple ways of creating links. External links are recognized automagically: http://www.google.com or simply www.google.com - You can set link text as well: [[http://www.google.com|This Link points to google]]. Email addresses like this one: are recognized, too. Internal Internal links are created by using square brackets. You can either just give a pagename or use an additional link text . Internal links are created by using square brackets. You can either just give a [[pagename]] or use an additional [[pagename|link text]]. Wiki pagenames are converted to lowercase automatically, special characters are not allowed. You can use namespaces by using a colon in the pagename. You can use [[some:namespaces]] by using a colon in the pagename. For details about namespaces see namespaces . Linking to a specific section is possible, too. Just add the section name behind a hash character as known from HTML . This links to this Section . This links to [[syntax#internal|this Section]]. Notes: Links to existing pages are shown in a different style from nonexisting ones. DokuWiki does not use CamelCase to automatically create links by default, but this behavior can be enabled in the config file. Hint: If DokuWiki is a link, then it's enabled. When a section's heading is changed, its bookmark changes, too. So don't rely on section linking too much. Interwiki DokuWiki supports Interwiki links. These are quick links to other Wikis. For example this is a link to Wikipedia's page about Wikis: Wiki . DokuWiki supports [[doku>Interwiki]] links. These are quick links to other Wikis. For example this is a link to Wikipedia's page about Wikis: [[wp>Wiki]]. Windows Shares Windows shares like this are recognized, too. Please note that these only make sense in a homogeneous user group like a corporate Intranet . Windows Shares like [[\\server\share|this]] are recognized, too. Notes: For security reasons direct browsing of windows shares only works in Microsoft Internet Explorer per default (and only in the “local zone”). For Mozilla and Firefox it can be enabled through different workaround mentioned in the Mozilla Knowledge Base . However, there will still be a JavaScript warning about trying to open a Windows Share. To remove this warning (for all users), put the following line in conf/userscript.js : LANG.nosmblinks = ''; Image Links You can also use an image to link to another internal or external page by combining the syntax for links and images (see below) like this: [[http://www.php.net|{{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}}]] Please note: The image formatting is the only formatting syntax accepted in link names. The whole image and link syntax is supported (including image resizing, internal and external images and URLs and interwiki links). Footnotes You can add footnotes 1) by using double parentheses. You can add footnotes ((This is a footnote)) by using double parentheses. Sectioning You can use up to five different levels of headlines to structure your content. If you have more than three headlines, a table of contents is generated automatically – this can be disabled by including the string ~~NOTOC~~ in the document. Headline Level 3 Headline Level 4 Headline Level 5 ==== Headline Level 3 ==== === Headline Level 4 === == Headline Level 5 == By using four or more dashes, you can make a horizontal line: Images and Other Files You can include external and internal images with curly brackets. Optionally you can specify the size of them. Real size: Resize to given width: Resize to given width and height 2) : Resized external image: Real size: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}} Resize to given width: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png?50}} Resize to given width and height: {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png?200x50}} Resized external image: {{http://de3.php.net/images/php.gif?200x50}} By using left or right whitespaces you can choose the alignment. {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png}} {{wiki:dokuwiki-128.png }} {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png }} Of course, you can add a title (displayed as a tooltip by most browsers), too. {{ wiki:dokuwiki-128.png |This is the caption}} If you specify a filename (external or internal) that is not an image ( gif, jpeg, png ), then it will be displayed as a link instead. For linking an image to another page see Image Links above. Lists Dokuwiki supports ordered and unordered lists. To create a list item, indent your text by two spaces and use a * for unordered lists or a - for ordered ones. This is a list The second item You may have different levels Another item The same list but ordered Another item Just use indention for deeper levels That's it * This is a list * The second item * You may have different levels * Another item - The same list but ordered - Another item - Just use indention for deeper levels - That's it Also take a look at the FAQ on list items . Text Conversions DokuWiki can convert certain pre-defined characters or strings into images or other text or HTML . The text to image conversion is mainly done for smileys. And the text to HTML conversion is used for typography replacements, but can be configured to use other HTML as well. Text to Image Conversions DokuWiki converts commonly used emoticon s to their graphical equivalents. Those Smileys and other images can be configured and extended. Here is an overview of Smileys included in DokuWiki: 8-) 8-O :-( :-) =) :-/ :-\ :-? :-D :-P :-O :-X :-| ;-) ^_^ :?: :!: LOL FIXME DELETEME Text to HTML Conversions Typography: DokuWiki can convert simple text characters to their typographically correct entities. Here is an example of recognized characters. → ← ↔ ⇒ ⇐ ⇔ » « – — 640×480 © ™ ® “He thought 'It's a man's world'…” -> <- <-> => <= <=> >> << -- --- 640x480 (c) (tm) (r) "He thought 'It's a man's world'..." The same can be done to produce any kind of HTML , it just needs to be added to the pattern file . There are three exceptions which do not come from that pattern file: multiplication entity (640×480), 'single' and “double quotes”. They can be turned off through a config option . Quoting Some times you want to mark some text to show it's a reply or comment. You can use the following syntax: I think we should do it > No we shouldn't >> Well, I say we should > Really? >> Yes! >>> Then lets do it! I think we should do it No we shouldn't Well, I say we should Really? Yes! Then lets do it! Tables DokuWiki supports a simple syntax to create tables. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 1 Row 1 Col 2 Row 1 Col 3 Row 2 Col 1 some colspan (note the double pipe) Row 3 Col 1 Row 3 Col 2 Row 3 Col 3 Table rows have to start and end with a | for normal rows or a ^ for headers. ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ Heading 3 ^ | Row 1 Col 1 | Row 1 Col 2 | Row 1 Col 3 | | Row 2 Col 1 | some colspan (note the double pipe) || | Row 3 Col 1 | Row 3 Col 2 | Row 3 Col 3 | To connect cells horizontally, just make the next cell completely empty as shown above. Be sure to have always the same amount of cell separators! Vertical tableheaders are possible, too. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 2 Row 1 Col 3 Heading 4 no colspan this time Heading 5 Row 2 Col 2 Row 2 Col 3 As you can see, it's the cell separator before a cell which decides about the formatting: | ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ ^ Heading 3 | Row 1 Col 2 | Row 1 Col 3 | ^ Heading 4 | no colspan this time | | ^ Heading 5 | Row 2 Col 2 | Row 2 Col 3 | You can have rowspans (vertically connected cells) by adding ::: into the cells below the one to which they should connect. Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Row 1 Col 1 this cell spans vertically Row 1 Col 3 Row 2 Col 1 Row 2 Col 3 Row 3 Col 1 Row 2 Col 3 Apart from the rowspan syntax those cells should not contain anything else. ^ Heading 1 ^ Heading 2 ^ Heading 3 ^ | Row 1 Col 1 | this cell spans vertically | Row 1 Col 3 | | Row 2 Col 1 | ::: | Row 2 Col 3 | | Row 3 Col 1 | ::: | Row 2 Col 3 | You can align the table contents, too. Just add at least two whitespaces at the opposite end of your text: Add two spaces on the left to align right, two spaces on the right to align left and two spaces at least at both ends for centered text. Table with alignment right center left left right center xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxx xxxxxxxxxxxx This is how it looks in the source: ^ Table with alignment ^^^ | right| center |left | |left | right| center | | xxxxxxxxxxxx | xxxxxxxxxxxx | xxxxxxxxxxxx | Note: Vertical alignment is not supported. No Formatting If you need to display text exactly like it is typed (without any formatting), enclose the area either with tags or even simpler, with double percent signs %% . This is some text which contains addresses like this: http://www.splitbrain.org and **formatting**, but nothing is done with it. The same is true for //__this__ text// with a smiley ;-). This is some text which contains addresses like this: http://www.splitbrain.org and **formatting**, but nothing is done with it. The same is true for %%//__this__ text// with a smiley ;-)%%. Code Blocks You can include code blocks into your documents by either indenting them by at least two spaces (like used for the previous examples) or by using the tags or . This is text is indented by two spaces. This is preformatted code all spaces are preserved: like <-this This is pretty much the same, but you could use it to show that you quoted a file. Those blocks were created by this source: This is text is indented by two spaces. This is preformatted code all spaces are preserved: like <-this This is pretty much the same, but you could use it to show that you quoted a file. Syntax Highlighting DokuWiki can highlight sourcecode, which makes it easier to read. It uses the GeSHi Generic Syntax Highlighter – so any language supported by GeSHi is supported. The syntax uses the same code and file blocks described in the previous section, but this time the name of the language syntax to be highlighted is included inside the tag, e.g. or . /** * The HelloWorldApp class implements an application that * simply displays "Hello World!" to the standard output. */ class HelloWorldApp { public static void main ( String [ ] args ) { System . out . println ( "Hello World!" ) ; //Display the string. } } The following language strings are currently recognized: 4cs, 6502acme, 6502kickass, 6502tasm, 68000devpac, abap, actionscript-french, actionscript, actionscript3, ada, algol68, apache, applescript, asm, asp, autoconf, autohotkey, autoit, avisynth, awk, bascomavr, bash, basic4gl, bf, bibtex, blitzbasic, bnf, boo, c, c_loadrunner, c_mac, caddcl, cadlisp, cfdg, cfm, chaiscript, cil, clojure, cmake, cobol, coffeescript, cpp, cpp-qt, csharp, css, cuesheet, d, dcs, delphi, diff, div, dos, dot, e, epc, ecmascript, eiffel, email, erlang, euphoria, f1, falcon, fo, fortran, freebasic, fsharp, gambas, genero, genie, gdb, glsl, gml, gnuplot, go, groovy, gettext, gwbasic, haskell, hicest, hq9plus, html, html5, icon, idl, ini, inno, intercal, io, j, java5, java, javascript, jquery, kixtart, klonec, klonecpp, latex, lb, lisp, llvm, locobasic, logtalk, lolcode, lotusformulas, lotusscript, lscript, lsl2, lua, m68k, magiksf, make, mapbasic, matlab, mirc, modula2, modula3, mmix, mpasm, mxml, mysql, newlisp, nsis, oberon2, objc, objeck, ocaml-brief, ocaml, oobas, oracle8, oracle11, oxygene, oz, pascal, pcre, perl, perl6, per, pf, php-brief, php, pike, pic16, pixelbender, pli, plsql, postgresql, povray, powerbuilder, powershell, proftpd, progress, prolog, properties, providex, purebasic, pycon, python, q, qbasic, rails, rebol, reg, robots, rpmspec, rsplus, ruby, sas, scala, scheme, scilab, sdlbasic, smalltalk, smarty, sql, systemverilog, tcl, teraterm, text, thinbasic, tsql, typoscript, unicon, uscript, vala, vbnet, vb, verilog, vhdl, vim, visualfoxpro, visualprolog, whitespace, winbatch, whois, xbasic, xml, xorg_conf, xpp, yaml, z80, zxbasic Downloadable Code Blocks When you use the or syntax as above, you might want to make the shown code available for download as well. You can do this by specifying a file name after language code like this: myexample.php If you don't want any highlighting but want a downloadable file, specify a dash ( - ) as the language code: . Embedding HTML and PHP You can embed raw HTML or PHP code into your documents by using the or tags. (Use uppercase tags if you need to enclose block level elements.) HTML example: This is some inline HTML

And this is some block HTML

This is some inline HTML And this is some block HTML PHP example: echo 'A logo generated by PHP:'; echo ''; echo '(generated inline HTML)'; echo ''; echo ''; echo '
The same, but inside a block level element:
'; echo 'A logo generated by PHP:'; echo ''; echo '(inline HTML )'; echo ''; echo ''; echo '
The same, but inside a block level element:
'; Please Note : HTML and PHP embedding is disabled by default in the configuration. If disabled, the code is displayed instead of executed. RSS/ATOM Feed Aggregation DokuWiki can integrate data from external XML feeds. For parsing the XML feeds, SimplePie is used. All formats understood by SimplePie can be used in DokuWiki as well. You can influence the rendering by multiple additional space separated parameters: Parameter Description any number will be used as maximum number items to show, defaults to 8 reverse display the last items in the feed first author show item authors names date show item dates description show the item description. If HTML is disabled all tags will be stripped n [dhm] refresh period, where d=days, h=hours, m=minutes. (e.g. 12h = 12 hours). The refresh period defaults to 4 hours. Any value below 10 minutes will be treated as 10 minutes. DokuWiki will generally try to supply a cached version of a page, obviously this is inappropriate when the page contains dynamic external content. The parameter tells DokuWiki to re-render the page if it is more than refresh period since the page was last rendered. Example: {{rss>http://slashdot.org/index.rss 5 author date 1h }} Control Macros Some syntax influences how DokuWiki renders a page without creating any output it self. The following control macros are availble: Macro Description ~~NOTOC~~ If this macro is found on the page, no table of contents will be created ~~NOCACHE~~ DokuWiki caches all output by default. Sometimes this might not be wanted (eg. when the syntax above is used), adding this macro will force DokuWiki to rerender a page on every call Syntax Plugins DokuWiki's syntax can be extended by Plugins . How the installed plugins are used is described on their appropriate description pages. DokuWiki
DokuWiki is a standards compliant, simple to use
Wiki
, mainly aimed at creating documentation of any kind. It is targeted at developer teams, workgroups and small companies. It has a simple but powerful
Formatting Syntax
which makes sure the datafiles remain readable outside the Wiki and eases the creation of structured texts. All data is stored in plain text files – no database is required.
Read the
DokuWiki Manual
to unleash the full power of DokuWiki.

Downtime Reports
Please login in order to see the complete listing of wiki pages in the category

ICS Computing Support generates Downtime Reports for any significant service outages.  A downtime report is a document that records and analyzes periods when equipment, a system, or a project is not operational. It details the duration, cause, and impact of both planned and unplanned stops to help identify inefficiencies and improve performance.

DTR #103607: Power Outage - Data Center Offline
DTR #103607: Power Outage - Data Center Offline
DTR #103607: Power Outage - Data Center Offline
Summary
The campus experienced two power outages over the weekend.
First Outage Event:
Time Begun: Saturday, June 21, 2025 at 15:58
Time Ended: Saturday, June 21, 2025 at 16:09
Second Outage Event:
Time Begun: Saturday, June 22, 2025 at 13:03
Time Ended: Sunday, June 22, 2025 at 13:55
The main file system, tardigrade, had a failed blade. This resulted in home and shared directories for most users to be unavailable. Many critical service hosts had to be manually powered back on. The site hub.ics.uci.edu was also affected, and the service is currently unstable.
Message from Mike Bonomo, PE
Campus Partners,

I wanted to let you know the following buildings in your area had a brief power outage today (Sunday, June 22nd) from about 1:15pm to 2:00pm:

Social Science Tower
Social Science Lab
Social Science Hall
School of Business 1 & 2
Engineering Tower
Engineering Lecture Hall
Engineering Laboratory Facility
Cal IT2
Information & Computer Science 1 & 2

During this window the main breakers that connect campus to the SCE grid opened which islanded the campus. The UCI Cogen plant was online generating power which prevented a campus wide power outage however we were not able to support the entire campus load and the above listed buildings were automatically de-energized as part of the campus islanding system.

Cogen engineers and campus electricians were dispatched to the buildings to check essential systems but would not be aware of special equipment in offices or lab. Please let me know if you have any question.
Customers Affected
All ICS users

Team Members
Hans Wunsch
Du Tran
Vipada DeLeon
Lucia Diaz
Remediation
Steps the team took to provide fixes to immediate service outages.
The team went in to the data center to physically check on the servers and power back on those that needed.
Pure Storage is sending out replacement equipment with an engineer on Tuesday 06/23/2025.
Pure Storage was able to bring a failed blade back online on Sunday morning restoring 100% available space.
Started nfs-mountd process on nerve.ics.uci.edu.
Solution
Additional steps the team needs to take:
Need to test the UPS in rack B06 as this rack should've remained up while the generator kicks in.
Test tardigrade UPS. Tardigrade should not have been unavailabe during the power outage on 06/22.
Improve monitoring NFS storage servers.
Walk data center regularly to check for failed equipment.
Move non-critical data from tardigrade storage server (fast) to bodleian storage (not as fast).
Move Chen Li's K8S storage from nerve to bodleian.

DTR Report #101784 Power Event 3/12/2025 and 3/31/2025
DTR Report #101784 Power Event 3/12/2025 and 3/31/2025
DTR Report #101784 Power Event 3/12/2025 and 3/31/2025
Summary
Helpdesk Ticket #101784
Brief flucuation in power, observed as lights blinking once, caused devices in data center to reset which led to widespread loss of services for approximately 5 minutes. The first email report arrived at 11:11am but may have been delayed due to down services. Services were significantly restored by 11:16.  Twenty fives hosts were found to have lost power down during the event and it was necessary to power them up manually.
Time Begun: 3/12/2025 ~11am
Time Ended: 3/12/2025 11:16 am
A second event occured on 3/31. I'm adding those notes to this DTR.
Time Begun: 3/31/2025 ~8:50am
Time Ended: 3/31/2025 9am
The following equipment looks like it reset
3/12/2025
3023-c2960s-095.ics.uci.edu
3023-c2960g-080.ics.uci.edu
3023-c2960g-086.ics.uci.edu
3023-c2960g-086.ics.uci.edu
3/31/2025
ethiopia
3023-C2950G-046
From Campus
Wednesday, March 12, 2025 11:33 AM
Hi all, please be advised that we are currently experiencing a widespread power outage on the UCI campus.  Emergency Management will continue to monitor this event.  This email is just a heads-up and there is no CCT call planned at this time, but we may need to initiate one if the event continues. 
...
Appears  Bio Sci, Bren, and other areas.
...
Please stand by.
From Hieu Trinh @ 11:25 AM
Dear ISEB Occupants,

We want to inform you that there is currently a power surge affecting our building and the campus as a whole. While all electronics should still be operational thanks to our backup generator, we have noticed that the autoclave is displaying a warning in some of the labs.

As a precaution, we advise your teams to proceed with caution and temporarily pause any experiments within the fume hood until UCI Facilities can resolve the issue Or until the autoclave no longer gives you the warning. In addition to that, you will also feel the building’s central air system working at a different rate which can affect the building’s air pressure. Please take extra precautions when opening and closing doors.

We appreciate your cooperation and will provide updates as soon as we have more information. Thank you for your patience.
Customers Affected
All ICS managed services and research located in the data center was affected
All ICS stakeholders
RCIC/CRSP (Imam Toufique @ RCIC)

Differences from Netapp Data
CIFS shares aren't creating automatically. You have to manually create them and edit them if someone's home directory moves.
Quotas are handled automatically depending on the virtual volume location that a user is placed in.
Most things are gui driven on tron.lom.ics.uci.edu from be4.
Users must have to have a default group manually entered in if their cifs connection is to create files correctly. Otherwise when they create files it wil be owned by user:root in unix/linux.
NFSv4 is on but doesn't work with our environment yet, auto_home entries must have -vers=3 in their options field to correctly work on solaris machines.
SMBv2 was enabled but turned off because of bugs. Unknown when it can be turned on again.
BlueArc support will give a release info on each version of the Titan software on demand but don't have it posted anywhere online.

There is a current project plan to get add 18TB of IGB space to the tainer_sas_pool and also 80 more TB of sata space to the tainer_sata_pool. Currently the process is:
Transfer all the WFS-1 file systems to the new storage pools of tainer_sas_600_pool and tainer2_sata formatted as WFS-2.
Add old stgpool tainer_sas_pool to tainer_sas_600_pool.
Rename pool to be tainer_sas_pool.
Add old stgpool tainer_sata_pool to tainer2_sata
Rename pool to be tainer_sas_pool.
Naming schemes
Storage pools
Type and speed for these bunch of disks, ie:
tainer sata -
tainer_sata_pool
tainer sas -
tainer_sas_pool
hds sata -
hsata_pool
igb hds fibre -
igb_hfc_pool
Filesystems
Can be extended but not shrunk. Only igb or ics have purchased space
ownershipOfDisks#_pool
igb0_tainer_sas
ics0_tainer_sata
Virtual Volumes
These are like qtrees in our NetApp Filer. Here we can specify quotas for the virtual volume based on the size, users, or groups. We can also do a file limit count for each of these quotas.
Set the option for hard limit so the user can see their usage with the quota command on Solaris and probably with df.
CIFS/NFS Exports
cifs exports -
'username
- how shares are exported out from tron. This is done at a per user level. Maybe a better way is to share a directory up like we do in nfs and then somehow in the ad_sync script make it allow for subdirectories on the home assignment?
kohlerr
nfs exports -
filesytem uniquename
- usually the directory up above people's stuff so we can just have one mount. Include the details of the location to make it unique and we can see where it is located without having to look on tron.
igb0_tainer_sas
Logging In
Tron is available for management login from the lom network.
Web
The web interface is available from any host on the lom network such as berrylium.  Goto
https://tron.lom.ics.uci.edu
, login as admin.
ssh
Login as the user `manager`.  The password will appear on your password list.  The ssh console is available from any host with an interface on the lom network (ie. ldap0).
If loggin in through an interactive shell, you will recieve a menu.  Choosing 1 will put you onto the tron storage itself.  Choose q will put you into the SMU.
serial port
On neuromancer, run minicom.  Connection settings:
Serial Device: /dev/ttyS0
Bps/Par/Bits: 1152000 8N1
SMU
SMU stands for the Server Management Unit and serves as a waypoint to tron from outside and a webserver to click around.
All the console commands from the SMU to the titan will require a -u supervisor -p password
Add a cifs export
Cifs exports are created on a per Active Directory user, so that I could access tron with \\tron.ics.uci.edu\kohlerr
Here's the command to add a cifs share from the command line interface on the SMU:
pssc -u supervisor -e 1 192.0.2.2 cifs-share add luser igb1_tainer_sata \\igb_home\\luser
Might only have to make sure the user's primary group is set in active directory. Testing this right now. Before with primary-group and linux samba server, tron would override the groups if you did a force group in the smb.conf.
Add a nfs export
NFS exports are created at the filesystem level and virtual volume level.
Use the smu web interface for this part of the
Make sure you have the right file system selected
Our nfs share options will be like the following for the filesystem level (i.e. dean0_tainer_sas):
trEowe_hosts(rw,no_root_squash,sec=sys)
Our nfs share options will be like the following for the virtual volume level (i.e. dean0_tainer_sas_staff:
trEowe_hosts(rw,no_root_squash,sec=sys)
nfs_clients(rw,sec=sys)
linux_nfs_clients(rw,sec=sys)
Add the NFS export to Apple ou
Just like with data and other nfs servers, if you want people to access the location on a Mac, you need to add an entry to our ldap. Our Titan 2200 only supports Leopard and newer too:
Make an ldif with your nfs export:
dn: cn=tron.ics.uci.edu:/ics0_nsas_support,cn=mounts,ou=apple,dc=ics,dc=uci,dc=edu
cn: tron.ics.uci.edu:/ics0_nsas_support
vfsOpts: net
vfsLinkDir: /Network/Servers
vfsType: nfs
objectClass: top
objectClass: mount
objectClass: ics-macuser
Now add it with something like:
ldapadd -v -h ldap0.ics.uci.edu -D uid=kohlerr,ou=People,dc=ics,dc=uci,dc=edu -f /home/kohlerr/solaris/scripts/ldap/apple_mount.ldif
Backups
The SMU automatically zips up files of all the configurations in the following location on the SMU:
/var/opt/smu/archive/smu-backup/10.195.1.5
I will write a script on william-hartnell to try to grab this information using rsync, for now I do the following:
root@william-hartnell 09:28:40 /home/icsdb/tron 
$ rsync -av --delete-during --rsync-path=/usr/bin/rsync manager@tron.lom.ics.uci.edu:/var/opt/smu/archive/smu-backup/10.195.1.5 /home/icsdb/tron/backup
Data Migrator
This is a tool when used, can offload data that meet certain criteria to a different filesystem. Usually this will be going from a higher tier of storage to a lower one.
Backing out of a migration
To get out of a migration, we have to get all the file back from the target file system back on the source file system.
Get rid of the policies and schedules through the SMU gui.
Once that's done we can try to remove the Data Migration Path, but it will most likely fail.
Now get on the Titan's cli and use the
reverse-migrate
command to get all the things we want back:
vn 1
selectfs test_dm_source
ls
reverse-migrate --file-system test_dm_source /pathToDirectoryTreeOrFile
In the event log it will tell you when it's done. Use the
reverse-migrate
command again to watch the progress. You can also queue different paths and it will do one at a time.
Becoming Root
Use the `su` command at the prompt, enter the UNIX DMZ password from the password list.
Reading Logs
Become root, cd to /var/log.
Running remote commands on Tron
You can use the ssc or pssc commands.  The pssc command is required for performing EVS specific commands:
ssc -u supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.2 filesystem-list
pssc -u supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile  -e 1 192.0.2.2 snapshot-list –file-system ics0_nsas
Titan Head Unit
192.0.2.2
The login username is
supervisor
and is set to the same password as data's root.
The titan head unit is the device does all the processing, nfs, and cifs work. There is a command line interface that you can use, but you have to go through the SMU to get on the internal private network.
Once you get a shell into the SMU, you can use the siconsole command to get inside the Titan.
Another way is to the SMU web interface and find the console:
Home -> SMU Administration -> SSHTerm
Quotas
On the Titan, there's several ways to do quotas. By user, group, virtual volume, or the filesystem.
A nice feature is that we can set up a default quota for a certain virtual volume or filesystem so we don't have to manage every single user if we don't want to using the
default-quota set
command.
default-quota set --usage-limit 6000M --usage-hard-limit yes user dean0_tainer_sas staff
If you change the default-quota, it happens instantly, but the SMU webpage might still show the old value. If you click on the details of one specific one and go back it should update. Otherwise you can download the quotas to check or do a
quota list fs
to double check.
Also to note, for place where users have unlimited usage, we may want to still setup a quota with size of 0 so we can track and see how much files/usage that people are using in that virtual volume.
Quota reporting
From the admin page on tron.lom.ics.uci.edu, you can dial in to
Virtual Volumes and Quotas
and choose the vv to look at. Right now it looks like there is a bug in reporting all the quotas from the gui. Doing the command line is recommended.
From tron itself, you can use the command
quota list <filesystem>
to get a list of everyone on that file system.
Snapshots
Show snapshots
is a setting that you can turn on and off per nfs and cifs share.
If the option is checked, it will be located in the following location in all directories:
windows = ~snapshot
unix/linux = .snapshot
If you uncheck this option, then users will not be able to cd into this directory in unix/linux and also from windows if you change the cifs option.
If using quotas, then snapshot data will not be be included in the report for quota.
LDAP auto_home auto_extra
For the moment, nfsv4 is not working but is enabled. So that means for our auto_extra and auto_home entries we have put the prefix:
-vers=3
An example:
-vers=3 tron.ics.uci.edu:/ics0_nsas_support/home/kohlerr
The following is a sample ldif for a home directory move:
dn: uid=coby,ou=People,dc=ics,dc=uci,dc=edu
changetype: modify
replace: mountDirectory
mountDirectory: /Network/Servers/tron.ics.uci.edu/ics1_fsas_support/coby


dn: cn=coby,nismapname=auto_home,dc=ics,dc=uci,dc=edu
changetype: modify
replace: nisMapEntry
nisMapEntry: -vers=3 tron.ics.uci.edu:/ics1_fsas_support/coby
Allow Office on Mac to save properly to the Desktop
The new version of Word has a problem when saving documents to network folders. Users with network home directories have a problem with Save or Autosave from Word 2004 11.2 (Office Service Pack 2) on OSX 10.4.2, and see an error message saying “Word cannot save this document due to a naming or permissions error on the destination volume.” The first attempt to save succeeds; subsequent attempts to save, or autosave, will fail with the above error message.
The fault occurs unless a folder called .TemporaryItems has been created at the root level of any mounted volume containing the saved file; so if the user's home directory is contained in an AFP share called Homes, then there has to be a directory called .TemporaryItems in the Homes folder on the server. If the home directory is on another local volume called UserData, there has to be a directory called .TemporaryItems at the root of that volume.
Since .TemporaryItems is a dot file, it will not be visible in the Finder. When the application is able to create this .TemporaryItems directory, it is world writable and sticky. For improved security, as long as all users are in a common group like “staff,” then the folder can be restricted to writing by the group instead.
$ cd /path/to/volume/or/sharepoint
 $ sudo mkdir .TemporaryItems
 $ chgrp istaff .TemporaryItems
 $ chmod 1770 .TemporaryItems
This seems to fix the problem, not just for Word, but PowerPoint and the other Office apps.
Primary Group problems
There are some instances where the primary group of a user is needed.
linux nfs wrong primary group
If the user has a primary group in LDAP that is not showing up when they create files on linux and tron, then they might have a primary-group entry set up on tron that is replacing it. This may happen on linux samba servers too:
To remove:get on the cli from tron.lom.ics.uci.edu from a server that can access the lom network like hawk-v1 (ssh manager@tron.lom.ics.uci.edu):
Tron:$ Please choose a server, or type 'q' to exit to a bash shell.
Server: UCI
Tron:$ primary-group-list UCI-ICS\vishalrp
Tron:$ primary-group-delete UCI-ICS\vishalrp
Set the primary group in active directory so that  their files created through CIFS via tron.ics.uci.edu will not have the  group root on them.  However, by default, the primary group for users  are Domain Users so their files will be created group owned by root via  CIFS.
If their primary group is not set in AD, set it via:
Tron:$ primary-group-set UCI-ICS\vishalrp UCI-ICS\grad
There is also scripts that will do this in /home/manager/bin

Upgrade
First upgrade the SMU first, then you can use it to update the titan with the .pkg file.
SMU Upgrade
Since we are using 6.1, we need to use the command line interface to do the upgrade. At version 6.5, we can use the web interface.
Download the iso for our version from
ftp.bluearc.com
Get the iso that ends with ba.iso
scp it to the home directory on tron.lom
scp /tmp/tSMUsetup_eaglecrest_1683.05_ba.iso manager@tron.lom.ics.uci.edu:/home/manager
Become root and mount the iso
su - root
mount -o loop /home/manager/SMUsetup_eaglecrest_1683.05_ba.iso /mnt
Run the following executable and after 10 minutes the smu will automatically reboot.
/mnt/autorun
Titan Upgrade
Be sure to first upgrade the SMU if there is a necessary upgrade via the SMU. It will ask for an iso so download the iso version that's just regular BA (not BANS).
You can upgrade the Titan firmware by going in to SMU
GUI
.
First let's check the server space by going to:
Home > Server Settings > Firmware Package Management
Here we can delete old packages to free up some space if we need it. At the top it'll say which pkg we are currently using so be sure not to delete that one!
Once you know there's enough space, go ahead and go to:
Home > Server Settings > Server Upgrade Selection
Click OK for the managed server, browse for the package and click apply. The .pkg file is the Titan firmware.
Here are my notes from the last upgrade:
prep ahead
moved the cables so that it can reach the c4948
configure port channel on c4948 for 6 ports
update the smu with iso after midnight snapshots (only this time for the version 6.1 to 7.0 big jump version change)
upload the pkg using new smu (make sure enough room, at least 100MB)
made sure had a recent copy of the smu backups from /var/opt/smu/archive/smu-backup

before reboot
go into machine room with laptop
zerov0 sendmail stop
skellingtonv0 sendmail stop
hannibalv0 /etc/init.d/mysql.server stop

reboot from tron smu
swap out the cables into the c4948 ports 21-26

make sure it comes back up with ip otherwise put it back
once it is talking again, then start the msyql, sendmail on above servers
Should make a note to warn IGB to stop their db's as well. It killed some of theirs.

Adding an IGB Filesystem
Be sure that they have enough space to create the filesystem. We have a split relationship with the amount of data on the AMS2500.
Filesystem name = igb#_storage_pool where # increments by 1 on each new one:
igb10_tainer_sata
igb1_tainer_sas
Find out if we need to create a 32KB or 4KB filesystem. If there is going to be just huge files only (> 5MB), then 32KB is better, otherwise choose 4KB.
Create a virtual volume at the root for the for the project or user and add jdhayes@ics.uci.edu as the e-mail contact.
sequser_vv
dock_vv
Create a nfs share which is the name of the filesystem with
trEowe_hosts(rw,no_root_squash,sec=sys)
nfs_clients(rw,sec=sys)
linux_nfs_clients(rw,sec=sys)
Set up snapshots and schedule as needed.
Set up backups if needed.
Moving a new user to Tron
Check for any dovecot shared accounts in the move. If their directory is 0 during the move then everyone will lose access to dovecot.
Make sure a virtual volume is created where the user will be put.
Create a cifs share for the user.
Set the primary-group for the user in active directory.
Check the permissions of their current home directory.
Make sure there is an apple-ou for the location and 711 up to their home directory.
Create .TemporaryItems in the mount to bypass office problems on OSX.
Make sure snapshots are running.
Make sure accessible through nfs.
Set up a quota based on how much it's using on Tron, might be different than data's quotas. Or use the default quota for the virtual volume.
Make sure there are no cifs sessions on data for the particular user, if so check if there are any files open.
Make sure if the user is a mac user that their macs are turned off or at least logged off. Reboot will fix any initial problems mounting.
Create an ldif for their autohome and mountDirectory and using -vers=3.
Make sure perms are right on tron.
Run the ad_sync.pl after applying the ldif to update their profile in active directory for their h:.
DWB/DRB Notes from Bluearch (not  Hitachi)
DWB/DRB document attached for greater detail.  Essentially, before you add more system drives to an existing storage pool, you'll want to make sure that all the Pool spaced is used by either expanding existing filesystems or creating a dummy filesystem.
Also before you add storage to an existing pool (and something to be aware of when creating a new pool) is that you'll want to make sure you expand in the same amount of SD's per stripeset.  A stripeset contains a group of SD's in which it writes to within a span.  You'll want to keep the number of SD's per STRIPESET within a Pool/span as close in size as possible.
For example this command can be run or found in the showall.txt of the sidiagnostics.  span-dump-cod –terse
The output below is from your system from your last diag.
This span(pool) contains one stripset (Set 0) in which that set contains 6 SD's (SD 32 through 36).  When you expand that pool, you'll want to match as closely as possible the number of SD's in the original stripset.
With each expansion of that pool you'll want to repeat the rule for using up all pool space, then expanding with equal SD's etc.
Hope that makes sense.

Running local stats
The following script will run some relevant stats.  This is run from the SMU.
echo ""
echo "*** initial performance stats"

echo ""
echo "*** Resetting counters"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 nfs-stats -r
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 connstats -r
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 perf -r
echo ""

sleep 3900
echo ""
echo "*** Post nfs-stats"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 nfs-stats
echo ""
echo "***  Post connections stats"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 connstats
echo ""
echo "*** Post perf stats"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 perf
echo ""
echo "*** TCP connection statistics from 1500-1600"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 tcp-per-connection-statistics-show
echo ""
echo "*** Connection Listings"
echo ""
ssc -u  supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 tcp-per-connection-statistics-show
> /tmp/connection.$$
foreach connection in `cat connection.$$ | awk '{print $1}' | egrep '^[0-9]+' | sort -u `;do
        ssc -u supervisor -F /tmp/pwfile 192.0.2.3 connection -s -c $connection
done
rm /tmp/connection.$$

Windows 8 SMB2 Clients
*Windows 8 SMB2 clients will not be supported by the Titan.
"For the future, we will support SMB2 signing on Angel-2 11.1.3200.00 and newer.  But firmware Angel-2 11.1.3200.00 is not supported on Titan hardware."
*HDS recommends:
1. Disable SMB2 so the Windows 9 client will use SMB1 to communicate with our HNAS server instead of SMB2.
or
2. Add a registry item to the Windows 8 client machine:
\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanWorkstation\Parameters\RequireSecureNegotiate, type DWORD, value 0.
*Choose option 2.  All other Win8 clients should map through samba.ics.uci.edu going forward.
Sparse directories message

cifs share gives access denied but works via samba
This is probably caused by the nis maps don't recognize the uid of the user's cifs share.
You can check this several ways:
On the tron admin webpage, you can go to File Services → User Mappings. Search for username and make sure to check mark Show Discovered Information
On tron's cli (not bash shell), you can use
ypmatch  -k username passwd.byname
What we need is a numerical UID from either approach. The first way will just give the name and no ID while the second would just return a complete error if the user isn't in there.
Solution
: Check ldap0 in the
/var/yp
folder and make sure the script for dumping ldap into nis is running. Also check root's crontab for it to see if it's scheduled or maybe just has run yet. It should look something like this:
root@nis0
  -  crontab -l
10,40 * * * * [[ -x /var/yp/run-hourly.sh ]] && /var/yp/run-hourly.sh > /dev/null
quota not showing up
When looking at the user quotas in a virtual volume, couldn't find the user in question.
Look up the user id.
Run the following command on tron cli:
user-mappings-list --unix-id 4257
If it's the wrong username. Check nis with the correct uid:
ypmatch -k cpswiki passwd.byname
It's probably a cached unix name to id, just refresh it with:
user-mappings-refresh --unix-id 4257
